_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to content/explanations/{skill_id}/{depth}.json. Remove all _comment fields.
title: Short, evocative title. E.g. 'Backpropagation — Teaching by Mistake'
key_insight: One sentence. The single most important takeaway. Should be memorable.
body:
## First Heading

Start with the intuition, not the definition. Use an analogy.

## Why It Works

Explain the underlying mechanism.

```python
# Include a minimal code example
x = example_code()
```

## AI Connection

Explicitly connect to AI/ML engineering.
common_mistakes: First common mistake — something learners almost always get wrong
Second common mistake — a subtle confusion or edge case
Third common mistake — a practical pitfall in ML code
mini_exercise: A short, doable task that takes 2-5 minutes. Should use Python if possible.
real_world_usage: One paragraph. Where is this used in production AI systems? Be specific: name a company, tool, paper, or framework.
explain_back_prompt: A question that forces the learner to reconstruct the concept in their own words.

build_task.title: Short title for the build task
build_task.context: One sentence: why this build task matters.
build_task.expected_output: Describe what a correct implementation produces.
build_task.hint: Optional: one hint for when the learner is stuck.
build_task.starter_code:
# Optional: scaffold code
def your_function():
    # TODO: implement this
    pass

_depth_guide.beginner: Use analogies. No jargon. Code examples 5-10 lines max.
_depth_guide.mid: Technical terminology. Real ML patterns. Code 10-30 lines.
_depth_guide.advanced: Production concerns. Performance. Research connections. Real tools/papers.
